subject: Revise dcae bootstrap version
commit_message:
Revise dcae bootstrap version

New bootstrap released for Son-handler
blueprint updates

Change-Id: I3bf4d177a6827ff6afe36a0d2130ccdc797ac099
Signed-off-by: Vijay Venkatesh Kumar <vv770d@att.com>
Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-2216
Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-2200

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

many bootstrapped containers are not running, preventing DCAE HealthCheck to be OK

Patch Set 1:

Sylvain - I've verified this new version in windriver/dcae tenant and deployment was successful. This possibly transient gating issue. Could you share bootstrap logs or retrigger oom/gating again?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> Sylvain - I've verified this new version in windriver/dcae tenant and deployment was successful. This possibly transient gating issue. Could you share bootstrap logs or retrigger oom/gating again?

The bootstrap log shows that CM is returning "internal error" responses (500) for some (but not all) of the API requests that bootstrap is making.   We've seen this before, but only in the gating environment.  Unfortunately the log doesn't provide any insight into why CM is returning those responses.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > Sylvain - I've verified this new version in windriver/dcae tenant and deployment was successful. This possibly transient gating issue. Could you share bootstrap logs or retrigger oom/gating again?
> 
> The bootstrap log shows that CM is returning "internal error" responses (500) for some (but not all) of the API requests that bootstrap is making.   We've seen this before, but only in the gating environment.  Unfortunately the log doesn't provide any insight into why CM is returning those responses.

Jack - I agree this is transient issue. Btw, where do you find the bootstrap logs from the gating run? If the CM logs are also available, possibly we could check why it returned 500?

Patch Set 1:

> Jack - I agree this is transient issue. Btw, where do you find the bootstrap logs from the gating run? If the CM logs are also available, possibly we could check why it returned 500?

The bootstrap logs are here:
https://gating-results.onap.eu/results//107253-1/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s/pod-onap-dcae-bootstrap-765c5499dc-hh9nr-dcae-bootstrap-logs.html

The interesting logs from CM are not written to stdout/stderr, they're in files in the container. (There are a number of logs from the different CM subsystems.)

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Jack - I agree this is transient issue. Btw, where do you find the bootstrap logs from the gating run? If the CM logs are also available, possibly we could check why it returned 500?
> 
> The bootstrap logs are here:
> https://gating-results.onap.eu/results//107253-1/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s/pod-onap-dcae-bootstrap-765c5499dc-hh9nr-dcae-bootstrap-logs.html
> 
> The interesting logs from CM are not written to stdout/stderr, they're in files in the container. (There are a number of logs from the different CM subsystems.)

Thanks for the pointer.  Yes, I notice error on bootstrap log - dont see much info on cloudify logs.

Sylvain/Krzysztof - Can you retrigger the gate job to verify again?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: -Code-Review
> 
> I don't understand why db replica is not up (logs says "ready") and not sure it's related to this patch (although other patches doesn't have the issue)

The log doesn't provide much information.  The readiness check for this is just a tcp connection to the postgres port (5432).  The k8s event log might have some indication of the reason for the readiness failure.

The dcae-db is brought up independently from the bootstrap container.  In fact, the bootstrap container has a readiness check that waits for dcae-db.
